list.name: Changes
list.comment:
Update task completion mutations, house queries, and documentation
- Modify `complete_task` and `uncompleted_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to accept an optional `user_id`, allowing actions for specific users while defaulting to the authenticated user. - Update `get_house_by_id` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to map and populate the `current_user_role` field for the authenticated user. - Revamp the API Usage section in `README.md` to include comprehensive tables of all available GraphQL queries and mutations, along with details on role management and authentication.

task.summary: Default task
init commit

Refactor GraphQL structure and add database setup
- Modularize GraphQL implementation by splitting schema, types, queries, and mutations into `app/graphql/`. - Add SQLAlchemy configuration in `app/database.py` and define `Task` model in `app/models.py`. - Update `main.py` to import the schema from the new location. - Add `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` for PostgreSQL setup. - Remove obsolete `schema.py`.

Refactor GraphQL structure and add database setup
- Modularize GraphQL implementation by splitting schema, types, queries, and mutations into `app/graphql/`. - Add SQLAlchemy configuration in `app/database.py` and define `Task` model in `app/models.py`. - Update `main.py` to import the schema from the new location. - Add `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` for PostgreSQL setup. - Remove obsolete `schema.py`.

Add Alembic for database migrations
- Configure Alembic with `alembic.ini` and `env.py`. - Generate initial migration for `Task` table. - Add migration to remove redundant index from `Task` table.

Add user management functionality
- Define `User` model in `app/models.py` with `id`, `email`, `hashed_password`, and `is_active` fields. - Implement GraphQL `User` types, queries, and mutations for user data handling. - Add Alembic migration to create `users` table.

Add house and task management features
- Introduce `House` and `TaskCategory` models in `app/models.py` with necessary relationships. - Define GraphQL types for `House`, `TaskCategory`, and `Assignment`. - Create Alembic migrations for new tables and relationships: - Add `houses` table and relationship with `tasks`. - Add `task_categories` table and relationship with `tasks`. - Add `assignments` table and relationships with `users` and `tasks`. - Update existing GraphQL types and models to include new relationships.

Add GraphQL mutations and utility for house creation
- Add `HouseMutations` to support house creation via GraphQL. - Implement `_generate_invite_code` utility in `house_service.py` for unique invite codes. - Update GraphQL schema to include house-related mutations. - Introduce `field(default_factory=list)` for safer list initialization in `GraphQL` types. - Add `create_task_category` mutation for task category handling.

Refactor GraphQL API to use dependency injection for database sessions
- Replace `SessionLocal` with database dependency injection using `Depends` and `context_getter`. - Update GraphQL queries and mutations to support the new context-based approach. - Simplify `main.py` with centralized database session management. - Adjust task, user, and house mutations/queries for `Info` dependency.

Add `name` field to `User` model and enhance house-related GraphQL features
- Add `name` column to `users` table via Alembic migration. - Update `User` GraphQL type, queries, and mutations to include `name`. - Enhance `House` functionality in GraphQL: - Add `get_house_by_invite_code` query. - Introduce `add_user_to_house` mutation. - Refactor `house_service` to include `generate_unique_invite_code` utility. - Update schema to include `HouseQueries` and refactored `HouseMutations`.

feat: Implement user authentication with JWT • Add user registration with password hashing using passlib. • Implement a login mutation that returns a JWT token on successful authentication. • Create an auth.py service to handle JWT token creation. • Add user_login.py service for user login logic. • Introduce UserError and AuthPayload types for clearer GraphQL responses. • Configure CORS middleware to allow frontend requests. • Centralize environment variables in a config.py file.

Add user context and enhance house-related GraphQL functionality
- Include `user_id` in GraphQL request context for authentication. - Add `get_house_by_user` query to retrieve user-associated houses. - Introduce `HouseError` type and enhance GraphQL mutations for better error handling. - Update `create_house` mutation to associate newly created houses with users. - Decode JWT tokens in `auth.py` to extract user information.

Add token expiration checks and enhance house-related GraphQL features
- Handle expired JWT tokens in `auth.py` and enhance `decode_access_token` to return `token_expired` status. - Update GraphQL context in `main.py` to include `token_expired` flag. - Add checks for token expiration in `create_house` and `get_house_by_user` mutations/queries. - Introduce `join_house_by_invitation_code` mutation for joining a house using an invite code. - Improve user and house associations with support for nested user data in `get_house_by_user`.

Add task recurrence, assignments, and completion tracking
- Introduce `TaskRecurrence`, `TaskLife`, and `TaskCompletion` models with corresponding Alembic migration. - Update GraphQL types to include new task-related entities and relationships. - Refactor `Task` model to remove `is_completed` field and add `weight` attribute. - Remove deprecated `TaskCategory` and `Assignment` models. - Add `get_house_by_id` query to improve house querying logic. - Enhance task management features with support for recurring tasks and completion tracking.

Update GraphQL schema, queries, and mutations for task recurrence and assignments
- Add `TaskRecurrenceQueries` to GraphQL schema for fetching task recurrence data. - Update `create_task` mutation to include recurrence, house, and assignment details. - Refactor GraphQL types and models for enhanced task relationship management. - Remove deprecated `TaskCategory` mutation and associated logic.

Enhance task GraphQL API with assignments and completions
- Update `Task` and `TaskLife` types in `app/graphql/types.py` to include `completions` and `assigned_users`. - Modify `get_tasks` and `get_house_by_id` in `app/graphql/queries.py` to populate nested task life data (recurrence, completions, assignments). - Replace `is_completed` field with `weight` in `Task` query mapping. - Add `assign_task_to_user` and `remove_user_from_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py`. - Refactor return types for `create_user` and `login` mutations to use explicit unions.

Add delete task mutation and enhance task error handling
- Define `TaskError`, `DeleteTaskSuccess`, and related unions in `app/graphql/types.py`. - Implement `delete_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove tasks by ID. - Update `assign_task_to_user` and `remove_user_from_task` to return specific `TaskError` or `UserError` instead of a generic message.

Implement task completion logic and current user query
- Add `complete_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to handle task completion events. - Create `app/services/period_key_service.py` to generate period keys based on task recurrence (Daily, Weekly, Monthly). - Add `get_me` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch authenticated user details. - Add explanatory comment for `period_key` in `app/graphql/types.py`.

Implement dynamic task completion checks and uncomplete mutation
- Add `is_completed` field resolvers to `Task` and `TaskLife` GraphQL types in `app/graphql/types.py` to determine status based on recurrence period. - Implement `uncompleted_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove existing task completions. - Define `UncompletedTaskSuccess` and `UncompletedTaskResult` types. - Remove static `is_completed` field assignment in `get_tasks` query in `app/graphql/queries.py`.

Add roles and role-based relationships for users and houses
- Define `Role` and `RoleHouseUser` models in `app/models.py` to establish user roles within houses. - Introduce `Role` and `RoleHouseUser` GraphQL types in `app/graphql/types.py`. - Update `User` and `House` GraphQL types to include `role_house_users` and `current_user_role` fields respectively. - Modify `get_me` and `get_house_by_user` queries in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch and resolve role data. - Add Alembic migration to create `roles` and `role_house_users` tables, and insert default "admin" and "user" roles.

Add role management mutation and unique constraint
- Add Alembic migration to enforce a unique constraint (`uq_role_house_user`) on `user_id` and `house_id` in the `role_house_users` table. - Implement `update_user_role` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to update a user's role in a house, including logic to prevent removing the last admin. - Add `RoleQueries` with a `get_roles` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch all available roles, and register it in `app/graphql/schema.py`. - Update `get_house_by_id` query to eagerly load and populate `role_house_users` for each user within a house.

Add `create_dummy_user_for_house` mutation
- Introduce mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to create a dummy user associated with a house, with auto-generated credentials. - Ensure validation for house existence and unique email address per house.

Add `remove_user_from_house` mutation
- Introduce mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove a user from a house. - Ensure validation for house and user existence before removal. - Automatically delete inactive users after removal.

Update task completion mutations, house queries, and documentation
- Modify `complete_task` and `uncompleted_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to accept an optional `user_id`, allowing actions for specific users while defaulting to the authenticated user. - Update `get_house_by_id` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to map and populate the `current_user_role` field for the authenticated user. - Revamp the API Usage section in `README.md` to include comprehensive tables of all available GraphQL queries and mutations, along with details on role management and authentication.

MESSAGE.value: init commit

Refactor GraphQL structure and add database setup
- Modularize GraphQL implementation by splitting schema, types, queries, and mutations into `app/graphql/`. - Add SQLAlchemy configuration in `app/database.py` and define `Task` model in `app/models.py`. - Update `main.py` to import the schema from the new location. - Add `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` for PostgreSQL setup. - Remove obsolete `schema.py`.

Add Alembic for database migrations
- Configure Alembic with `alembic.ini` and `env.py`. - Generate initial migration for `Task` table. - Add migration to remove redundant index from `Task` table.

Add user management functionality
- Define `User` model in `app/models.py` with `id`, `email`, `hashed_password`, and `is_active` fields. - Implement GraphQL `User` types, queries, and mutations for user data handling. - Add Alembic migration to create `users` table.

Add house and task management features
- Introduce `House` and `TaskCategory` models in `app/models.py` with necessary relationships. - Define GraphQL types for `House`, `TaskCategory`, and `Assignment`. - Create Alembic migrations for new tables and relationships: - Add `houses` table and relationship with `tasks`. - Add `task_categories` table and relationship with `tasks`. - Add `assignments` table and relationships with `users` and `tasks`. - Update existing GraphQL types and models to include new relationships.

Add GraphQL mutations and utility for house creation
- Add `HouseMutations` to support house creation via GraphQL. - Implement `_generate_invite_code` utility in `house_service.py` for unique invite codes. - Update GraphQL schema to include house-related mutations. - Introduce `field(default_factory=list)` for safer list initialization in `GraphQL` types. - Add `create_task_category` mutation for task category handling.

Add README for project setup and usage
- Provide project overview, tech stack, requirements, and API usage details. - Include setup instructions for dependencies, migrations, and server startup. - Outline project structure, environment variables, and testing information. - Add placeholders for license and dependency management files.

Refactor GraphQL API to use dependency injection for database sessions
- Replace `SessionLocal` with database dependency injection using `Depends` and `context_getter`. - Update GraphQL queries and mutations to support the new context-based approach. - Simplify `main.py` with centralized database session management. - Adjust task, user, and house mutations/queries for `Info` dependency.

Add `name` field to `User` model and enhance house-related GraphQL features
- Add `name` column to `users` table via Alembic migration. - Update `User` GraphQL type, queries, and mutations to include `name`. - Enhance `House` functionality in GraphQL: - Add `get_house_by_invite_code` query. - Introduce `add_user_to_house` mutation. - Refactor `house_service` to include `generate_unique_invite_code` utility. - Update schema to include `HouseQueries` and refactored `HouseMutations`.

feat: Implement user authentication with JWT • Add user registration with password hashing using passlib. • Implement a login mutation that returns a JWT token on successful authentication. • Create an auth.py service to handle JWT token creation. • Add user_login.py service for user login logic. • Introduce UserError and AuthPayload types for clearer GraphQL responses. • Configure CORS middleware to allow frontend requests. • Centralize environment variables in a config.py file.

Add user context and enhance house-related GraphQL functionality
- Include `user_id` in GraphQL request context for authentication. - Add `get_house_by_user` query to retrieve user-associated houses. - Introduce `HouseError` type and enhance GraphQL mutations for better error handling. - Update `create_house` mutation to associate newly created houses with users. - Decode JWT tokens in `auth.py` to extract user information.

Add token expiration checks and enhance house-related GraphQL features
- Handle expired JWT tokens in `auth.py` and enhance `decode_access_token` to return `token_expired` status. - Update GraphQL context in `main.py` to include `token_expired` flag. - Add checks for token expiration in `create_house` and `get_house_by_user` mutations/queries. - Introduce `join_house_by_invitation_code` mutation for joining a house using an invite code. - Improve user and house associations with support for nested user data in `get_house_by_user`.

Add task recurrence, assignments, and completion tracking
- Introduce `TaskRecurrence`, `TaskLife`, and `TaskCompletion` models with corresponding Alembic migration. - Update GraphQL types to include new task-related entities and relationships. - Refactor `Task` model to remove `is_completed` field and add `weight` attribute. - Remove deprecated `TaskCategory` and `Assignment` models. - Add `get_house_by_id` query to improve house querying logic. - Enhance task management features with support for recurring tasks and completion tracking.

Update GraphQL schema, queries, and mutations for task recurrence and assignments
- Add `TaskRecurrenceQueries` to GraphQL schema for fetching task recurrence data. - Update `create_task` mutation to include recurrence, house, and assignment details. - Refactor GraphQL types and models for enhanced task relationship management. - Remove deprecated `TaskCategory` mutation and associated logic.

Enhance task GraphQL API with assignments and completions
- Update `Task` and `TaskLife` types in `app/graphql/types.py` to include `completions` and `assigned_users`. - Modify `get_tasks` and `get_house_by_id` in `app/graphql/queries.py` to populate nested task life data (recurrence, completions, assignments). - Replace `is_completed` field with `weight` in `Task` query mapping. - Add `assign_task_to_user` and `remove_user_from_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py`. - Refactor return types for `create_user` and `login` mutations to use explicit unions.

Add delete task mutation and enhance task error handling
- Define `TaskError`, `DeleteTaskSuccess`, and related unions in `app/graphql/types.py`. - Implement `delete_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove tasks by ID. - Update `assign_task_to_user` and `remove_user_from_task` to return specific `TaskError` or `UserError` instead of a generic message.

Implement task completion logic and current user query
- Add `complete_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to handle task completion events. - Create `app/services/period_key_service.py` to generate period keys based on task recurrence (Daily, Weekly, Monthly). - Add `get_me` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch authenticated user details. - Add explanatory comment for `period_key` in `app/graphql/types.py`.

Implement dynamic task completion checks and uncomplete mutation
- Add `is_completed` field resolvers to `Task` and `TaskLife` GraphQL types in `app/graphql/types.py` to determine status based on recurrence period. - Implement `uncompleted_task` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove existing task completions. - Define `UncompletedTaskSuccess` and `UncompletedTaskResult` types. - Remove static `is_completed` field assignment in `get_tasks` query in `app/graphql/queries.py`.

Add roles and role-based relationships for users and houses
- Define `Role` and `RoleHouseUser` models in `app/models.py` to establish user roles within houses. - Introduce `Role` and `RoleHouseUser` GraphQL types in `app/graphql/types.py`. - Update `User` and `House` GraphQL types to include `role_house_users` and `current_user_role` fields respectively. - Modify `get_me` and `get_house_by_user` queries in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch and resolve role data. - Add Alembic migration to create `roles` and `role_house_users` tables, and insert default "admin" and "user" roles.

Add role management mutation and unique constraint
- Add Alembic migration to enforce a unique constraint (`uq_role_house_user`) on `user_id` and `house_id` in the `role_house_users` table. - Implement `update_user_role` mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to update a user's role in a house, including logic to prevent removing the last admin. - Add `RoleQueries` with a `get_roles` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to fetch all available roles, and register it in `app/graphql/schema.py`. - Update `get_house_by_id` query to eagerly load and populate `role_house_users` for each user within a house.

Add `create_dummy_user_for_house` mutation
- Introduce mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to create a dummy user associated with a house, with auto-generated credentials. - Ensure validation for house existence and unique email address per house.

Add `remove_user_from_house` mutation
- Introduce mutation in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to remove a user from a house. - Ensure validation for house and user existence before removal. - Automatically delete inactive users after removal.

Update task completion mutations, house queries, and documentation
- Modify `complete_task` and `uncompleted_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to accept an optional `user_id`, allowing actions for specific users while defaulting to the authenticated user. - Update `get_house_by_id` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to map and populate the `current_user_role` field for the authenticated user. - Revamp the API Usage section in `README.md` to include comprehensive tables of all available GraphQL queries and mutations, along with details on role management and authentication.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value:
Update task completion mutations, house queries, and documentation
- Modify `complete_task` and `uncompleted_task` mutations in `app/graphql/mutations.py` to accept an optional `user_id`, allowing actions for specific users while defaulting to the authenticated user. - Update `get_house_by_id` query in `app/graphql/queries.py` to map and populate the `current_user_role` field for the authenticated user. - Revamp the API Usage section in `README.md` to include comprehensive tables of all available GraphQL queries and mutations, along with details on role management and authentication.
